test_cannotReinitialize_succeeds().notice: Tests that:         1. The `initialized` flag of each contract is properly set to `type(uint64).max`,            signifying that the contracts are initialized.         2. The `initialize()` function of each contract cannot be called more than once.         3. Returns the correct error when attempting to re-initialize a contract.

Tests that:         1. The `initialized` flag of each contract is properly set to `type(uint64).max`,            signifying that the contracts are initialized.         2. The `initialize()` function of each contract cannot be called more than once.         3. Returns the correct error when attempting to re-initialize a contract.

devdoc.details: Ensures that the `initialize()` function on contracts cannot be called more than      once. Tests the contracts inheriting from `Initializable` from OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.
devdoc.title: InitializerOZv5_Test

documentation.text:
@notice The storage slot of the `initialized` flag in the `Initializable` contract from OZ v5.
 keccak256(abi.encode(uint256(keccak256("openzeppelin.storage.Initializable")) - 1)) & ~bytes32(uint256(0xff))

@notice Contains the address of an `Initializable` contract and the calldata
         used to initialize it.

@notice Contains the addresses of the contracts to test as well as the calldata
         used to initialize them.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (struct InitializerOZv5_Test.InitializeableContract storage ref[] storage pointer,struct InitializerOZv5_Test.InitializeableContract storage ref)

documentation.text:
@notice Tests that:
         1. The `initialized` flag of each contract is properly set to `type(uint64).max`,
            signifying that the contracts are initialized.
         2. The `initialize()` function of each contract cannot be called more than once.
         3. Returns the correct error when attempting to re-initialize a contract.
